title: memstead-plugin/v0 .memstead.toml
description: Top-level workspace manifest for a memstead-plugin/v0 workspace. The plugin reads this file to discover the registered vaults and any client- or plugin-namespaced configuration. The engine consumes only `vaults`; `schemas_dir` is retired and ignored (the engine loads workspace schemas from the fixed `.memstead/schemas/` path), and the four-primitive configs live at fixed `.memstead/{mediums,facets,projections,ingests}/` paths rather than via directory-pointer keys. The remainder is plugin-owned. Validation gates the plugin loader on the `format` version key. Pass-through tables under `[clients.*]` and `[plugin.*]` are accepted without further constraint so plugin extensions can declare their own keys.

format.description: Pinned format version. Producers must emit this exact string for v0; the plugin loader rejects any other value with a clear error listing supported versions.

vaults.description: Explicitly registered vault paths, relative to this file's directory. No auto-discovery. Runtime-created vaults are appended here by `memstead_vault_create`.

schemas_dir.description: Retired and ignored by the engine: workspace schemas are loaded from the fixed `.memstead/schemas/` path, and the engine warns if this key is present. Kept in the schema only so an older manifest that still carries it validates.

mutations.description: Engine-owned mutation policy. See WorkspaceToml in memstead-schema for accepted keys.

vault_management.description: Engine-owned vault lifecycle policy. Two array-of-tables — `[[vault_management.create]]` (per rule: `pattern` + `schemas[]`) and `[[vault_management.delete]]` (per rule: `pattern` only). Glob matching is gitignore-style over the composed candidate `<path>/<name>`. The legacy `allowed_create_patterns` / `allowed_delete_patterns` flat-list shape is rejected at parse.

clients.description: Pass-through namespace for client-specific configuration tables (e.g. `[clients.claude_code]`). Keys and shapes are owned by individual clients; the plugin loader does not constrain them.

plugin.description: Pass-through namespace for plugin-specific configuration tables (e.g. `[plugin.claude_code.outer_vcs]`). Keys and shapes are owned by individual plugins; the loader does not constrain them.
